metadata.title: Infectious Diseases Expert Examination-Selection Evaluation Set
metadata.purpose: Held-out open-response evaluation for the minimal examination-selection extension. Each item asks what an ID expert should examine, ask, collect, or test next from a presenting case before definitive results.
metadata.not_for_clinical_use: Research/evaluation scaffold only; not clinical software or patient care guidance.
metadata.source_policy: Per user preference, source URLs are not embedded per item. The item gold standards were reviewed against authoritative infectious-disease, microbiology, travel, STI, sepsis, and opportunistic-infection guidance.
metadata.recommended_prompt: What should an infectious diseases expert examine, ask, collect, or test next? Give a focused examination/testing plan only; do not provide treatment.
metadata.leakage_note: Hidden condition is included for judging and analysis; do not show hidden_condition to the doctor model.

items.presenting_case: A 72-year-old with diabetes is brought in with fever, confusion, RR 28, BP 86/52, mottled legs, and no obvious source. He has a urinary catheter and a new cough.
items.question: What should an infectious diseases expert examine, ask, collect, or test next? Give a focused examination/testing plan only; do not provide treatment.
gold_essential: two sets of blood cultures from separate venipuncture sites before antimicrobials if no substantial delay
serum lactate and basic organ dysfunction labs
source-directed cultures/specimens before antibiotics when feasible
source-directed imaging when focal signs are present
gold_focused_exam_or_history: full vital signs and shock assessment
focused source exam: chest, abdomen, skin/soft tissue, lines, urinary, CNS
review devices, recent procedures, antimicrobials, immune status
accepted_alternatives_or_second_line: respiratory viral testing/chest imaging if respiratory source
urinalysis and urine culture if urinary symptoms or no source
line cultures if central-line source suspected
dangerous_misses: missing occult source or source control clues
low_value_or_wrong: using procalcitonin alone to decide infection
ordering broad serology panels before cultures
delaying antimicrobials in shock to complete low-yield tests

items.question: What should an infectious diseases expert examine, ask, collect, or test next? Give a focused examination/testing plan only; do not provide treatment.

assess whether CT head is needed before LP based on focal deficit, papilledema, seizure, immunocompromise, or marked altered mental status

accepted_alternatives_or_second_line: meningitis/encephalitis PCR panel when available

dangerous_misses: missing blood cultures if LP delayed
low_value_or_wrong: CT head for everyone before LP regardless of risk
serum inflammatory markers as the main diagnostic test
delaying antibiotics for LP/CT when unstable

items.presenting_case: A 47-year-old has fever, confusion, new focal seizures, and odd behaviour after a week of headache.
items.question: What should an infectious diseases expert examine, ask, collect, or test next? Give a focused examination/testing plan only; do not provide treatment.
gold_essential: MRI brain preferred when feasible
lumbar puncture with CSF cell count, protein, glucose and HSV PCR
EEG if seizures or encephalopathy
basic evaluation for alternate encephalitis causes
gold_focused_exam_or_history: mental status, focal deficits, seizure history, temporal lobe signs
accepted_alternatives_or_second_line: repeat CSF HSV PCR if early negative but suspicion remains
VZV/enterovirus/arboviral PCR or serology guided by exposure and season
low_value_or_wrong: only CT head without CSF testing
routine serum HSV serology as diagnostic
waiting for MRI before CSF/empiric pathway when unsafe

CT neck if alternative deep-neck infection and stable
dangerous_misses: failure to assess for deep neck alternative
low_value_or_wrong: aggressive throat exam with tongue depressor in unstable patient
delaying airway evaluation for routine labs
outpatient swab-first approach

blood cultures if pyogenic abscess differential

assuming pyogenic abscess without amebic testing in endemic exposure
drainage of all uncomplicated abscesses
